content:
It was a privilege to serve as the Co-Chair in the Technical Sessions of the Two-Day National Seminar on "Women, Law and Social Change in India: Navigating Rights, Justice, Contemporary Challenges and Pathways to Empowerment," organized by Shree Krishna Jubilee Law College, Muzaffarpur, Bihar in hybrid mode.
The seminar provided an enriching platform for meaningful deliberations on women's rights, access to justice, legal reforms, and the evolving challenges in achieving gender equality. It was inspiring to engage with distinguished academicians, legal professionals, researchers, and students who contributed valuable insights toward advancing women's empowerment through law and policy.
I sincerely thank the organizers specially Dr. Manvendra Tripathi for the opportunity to be part of this important academic event and look forward to many more such scholarly engagements that promote dialogue, research, and social transformation.
